Build a mapping from raw pixels to unit-circle coordinates

Source: R/circular_mapping.R
build_unit_circle_mapping.Rd

Build a mapping from raw pixels to unit-circle coordinates

Usage

build_unit_circle_mapping(origin, reference, flip_y = TRUE)

Arguments

origin

Numeric vector of length 2 giving the circle origin (x, y).

reference

Numeric vector of length 2 giving the landmark on the perimeter that defines the zero-degree heading.

flip_y

Logical; if `TRUE` (default), invert the y-axis so positive values point upward (matching the unit-circle convention).

Value

A list containing origin metadata, a forward mapping (`map`) that converts raw pixels to unit-circle coordinates, and an inverse mapping (`inverse`) that converts back to the original pixel space.

On this page
